decisionMemorySummary.note: Aggregates Codex recommendation snapshots compared against final human workbench decisions. Missing historical Codex recommendations remain unavailable and are not inferred.

meta.note: Codex-first conservative review. Human QC still required before merge.

highRiskItems.reviewerReasoningSummary: Potential new question but local answer key is unknown; kept unresolved for human review.
highRiskItems.rejectedTopMatcherReason: Potential new question but local answer key is unknown; kept unresolved for human review.

highRiskItems.reviewerReasoningSummary: Visual arrow shows straight then right (meaning 'go straight or turn right') but source options list single actions only (no combined choice). Top machine candidates disagree (some indicate U-turn). Options set mismatch — cannot safely map to an existing qid or a single source letter.
highRiskItems.rejectedTopMatcherReason: Top matcher q0848 indicates a U-turn meaning which conflicts with the visual semantics (straight+right); candidate set / top match inconsistent with image.

highRiskItems.reviewerReasoningSummary: Statement ('must not use either headlights or rear position lights in sandstorm') appears questionable/contradictory to standard guidance (use low beams/hazards). No confident existing qid match; legal/regulatory correctness uncertain.

rejectedTopMatcherExamples.reviewerReasoningSummary: Top candidates conflict (q0304 Wrong vs q0303 Right) and matching signals are close; image semantics ambiguous re: priority — not enough confidence to pick existing qid.
Machine candidates show low/ambiguous agreement and option sets do not align cleanly with top matches (several action choices); cannot reliably map to existing qid.

Text-only legal item about driving during a renewal-deferral period; candidate prompts overlap but no clear exact match (low machine agreement). Require human legal verification or create-new if confirmed.
Potential new question but local answer key is unknown; kept unresolved for human review.

rejectedTopMatcherExamples.reviewerReasoningSummary: Image shows a white diamond/arrow road marking; machine candidates disagree between 'central circle' and 'cross-hatched/yellow grid'. Visual semantics ambiguous; defer to human reviewer.

Visual shows blue circle with red border and single diagonal — standard 'défense de stationner' (no parking). None of the top candidate QIDs clearly and unambiguously match this exact localized option ordering.

Image clearly shows a '110' speed-limit sign. q0480 has 110km/h as the correct numeric meaning; in this localized option ordering 110 corresponds to B. Some machine matches conflict, so moderate risk.
Green rectangular ETC lane sign with 'ETC' and arrow; localized option A ('voie de télépéage') matches the visual. No single existing QID clearly matches the exact sign/option ordering.

Asks penalty points for forged 'Carte Grise'. Machine candidates reference penalties for forged driver's license/plates with mixed signals. Legal/penalty mapping is ambiguous here — insufficient confidence to map to existing QID or pick an answer.
Scenario image and prompt ask who to yield to when going straight; correct behavior is to yield to vehicle from the right. q0503 expresses 'yield to vehicle from right' — localized option B matches.

ROW item: indicator depicts coolant/temperature icon; statement that it means coolant insufficient is true. No exact existing ROW QID unambiguously matched in top candidates, so create new ROW with 'Right'.

rejectedTopMatcherExamples.reviewerReasoningSummary: Sign shows horn in red prohibition circle — meaning 'no honking'. q0889 has 'no honking' as the correct meaning; source option C is 'défense de klaxonner'. Top machine candidate (q0657) had conflicting mapping, so flagged moderate risk.
rejectedTopMatcherExamples.rejectedTopMatcherReason: Top matcher q0657's suggested correct option conflicts with the visual meaning ('should honk' vs actual prohibition); candidates disagree.

rejectedTopMatcherExamples.reviewerReasoningSummary: Image clearly shows a '120' speed sign. q0399 has correctOptionText 120 and matches the visual. Top machine candidate q0367 suggested different value (90), so flagged moderate risk due to candidate conflict.
rejectedTopMatcherExamples.rejectedTopMatcherReason: Top matcher q0367 predicts 90km/h which conflicts with the visible '120' sign and with q0399 image candidate.

rejectedTopMatcherExamples.reviewerReasoningSummary: Source states unpaid fines cause penalty points to carry into next cycle; q0233 explicitly matches that behavior. Candidate ranking lower but semantics align.
Blue rectangular sign with three directional arrows = panneau de direction. No existing candidate qid clearly matches the simple 'type of sign' question.
Image shows a white vertical arrow pointing straight ahead on blue rectangular sign => 'aller tout droit' (A). Top machine candidate conflicted (right-turn), so create new to avoid mismapping.

Road markings (two forward arrows) do not normally instruct to accelerate; likely answer 'No' (B). However existing qids (q0219/q0220) show close but not exact parity — mapping uncertain so keep unresolved.

Yellow triangular warning sign with Chinese character likely indicates 'slow / pass slowly' or a specific hazard. Unclear which French option matches exactly (B vs C); machine candidate suggests 'passing slowly' but mapping to local options is ambiguous.

Statement ('must not use either headlights or rear position lights in sandstorm') appears questionable/contradictory to standard guidance (use low beams/hazards). No confident existing qid match; legal/regulatory correctness uncertain.

Question asks what to do before restarting from roadside; correct action is to observe surroundings. That meaning corresponds to q0154; in this localized option set it's option C.

items.reviewerReasoningSummary: Question asks licence ban after fleeing an accident constituting a crime; q0496's correct answer is 'for lifetime' which matches source option C ('Toute la vie').

Dashboard shows red battery symbol (+/-). Visual semantics and top candidate q0584 indicate charging/charging-circuit malfunction; that corresponds to source option D. There is a conflicting OCR/extracted key (B), so moderate risk.
Statement '50% over speed -> 3 points' is matched by q0280 which marks that claim as Wrong. Candidates support 'Wrong'. Legal specifics could vary; moderate confidence.

Sign shows horn in red prohibition circle — meaning 'no honking'. q0889 has 'no honking' as the correct meaning; source option C is 'défense de klaxonner'. Top machine candidate (q0657) had conflicting mapping, so flagged moderate risk.

items.reviewerReasoningSummary: Question about seat belt function; q0626's correct answer is 'to reduce injuries of driver and passengers', matching source option A.

Image clearly shows a '120' speed sign. q0399 has correctOptionText 120 and matches the visual. Top machine candidate q0367 suggested different value (90), so flagged moderate risk due to candidate conflict.

items.reviewerReasoningSummary: Statement 'prohibited to overtake on narrow road/bridge' matches q0074 which is True/Right. Candidates support this meaning.
Source states unpaid fines cause penalty points to carry into next cycle; q0233 explicitly matches that behavior. Candidate ranking lower but semantics align.
Blue rectangular sign with three directional arrows = panneau de direction. No existing candidate qid clearly matches the simple 'type of sign' question.
Image shows a white vertical arrow pointing straight ahead on blue rectangular sign => 'aller tout droit' (A). Top machine candidate conflicted (right-turn), so create new to avoid mismapping.

Road markings (two forward arrows) do not normally instruct to accelerate; likely answer 'No' (B). However existing qids (q0219/q0220) show close but not exact parity — mapping uncertain so keep unresolved.

Yellow triangular warning sign with Chinese character likely indicates 'slow / pass slowly' or a specific hazard. Unclear which French option matches exactly (B vs C); machine candidate suggests 'passing slowly' but mapping to local options is ambiguous.
Text states visibility range shortens at night and driver tires easily — matches q0101 semantics (Right/Yes). Good textual alignment.

Statement ('must not use either headlights or rear position lights in sandstorm') appears questionable/contradictory to standard guidance (use low beams/hazards). No confident existing qid match; legal/regulatory correctness uncertain.

Image: blue car parked with warning triangle behind. Whether 'no illegal act' is true depends on context (parking rules, triangle placement). Candidate qids show mixed matches; unable to safely map or decide correctness.
Dashboard icon shows yellow car with open hood — semantics match q0548 'indicates hood open'. Approve mapping; machine candidate answer inconsistent but visual semantics are clear.
Statement about tire blowout — do not brake abruptly; shift to low gear and use engine braking — matches q0088 (Right/Yes).

Question about narrow mountain road: correct action is 'reduce speed or stop and yield' which corresponds to Q0144 semantics. Machine candidate q0144 present; map to local option B (reduce speed/stop).

Yellow triangular warning sign with worker pictogram = roadworks. Top candidate q0888 has identical options and correct = construction zone → source option B ('tronson en travaux'). Strong image match.
Text asks which action leads to 200–2000 yuan fine and license revocation. Top candidate q0405 matches wording exactly; master correct = driving 50% over limit → source option B.

Ambiguous. Source asks if headrest effectively protects driver's head. Top candidate q0531 is related but wording/claim differs and its key indicates 'Wrong' for a slightly different statement. Cannot confidently map; keep unresolved.

Low-match scores and candidates are not direct matches. The correct legal answer (overtaking prohibited on which section) can't be inferred with high confidence from candidates or source alone; do not create or map a qid.
Prompt asks punishment for driving an out-of-service (write-off) vehicle. Top candidate q0404 correct = revoke license (master D); maps to source option B ('retrait du permis de conduire').
Statement says police may legally detain crashed vehicle to collect evidence. Top candidate q0212 matches prompt and marks statement True → source option A (Yes).
Asks where to change lane before intersection. Top candidate q0111 correct = area marked by broken lines per guide arrow → source option C corresponds to that wording.
Statement about ABS preserving steering during hard braking. Top candidate q0574 matches and marks statement True → source option A (Yes).

Source claims police do NOT have right to question drivers who remained but should have left; top candidate q0250 gives similar statement and labels it Wrong. That maps to source option B (No). Slight wording differences lower confidence.

Asks main influence of mountain roads; top candidate q0180 correct = long slopes, sharp curves, shorter visibility (master B) which corresponds to source option C. Good semantic match though scores modest.
